changes.title: PANIC-EXIT DISCIPLINE — DO NOT SELL INTO RED WITHOUT A REAL TRIGGER
changes.summary: Defines 4 valid triggers (binary event / thesis broken / market-wide risk-off / approaching SL) for any discretionary EXIT on a position trading at a loss but above its SL. Disqualifies narrative-style reasons ('rotation stalled', 'overhang', 'intraday rejection', 'capital preservation'). Recommends MODIFY-tighten-SL as the default tool when reduced exposure is desired without a clean trigger.

specific_trades.reason_text: Stock down -2.7% from entry 1810, now at 1754. Intraday rejection pattern... After +39% April rally, MarketsMOJO downgraded to Hold...
specific_trades.trigger_check: exit was AT-OR-PAST SL trigger (1761.82 < 1775); broker stop should already have fired

specific_trades.reason_text: PSU Bank index down -1.57% today, MAHABANK at -3.5% loss... Rs 7,500 cr equity raise overhang creates dilution risk. PSU bank rotation has stalled.
specific_trades.trigger_check: neither overhang nor sector rotation are post-entry information; both publicly known on Day-1; SL still 2.55 pp away

specific_trades.reason_text: Down -2.2% with Q4 results in T+2 (Apr 30) — Street expectations weak
specific_trades.trigger_check: valid rule 11 binary event trigger; defensible under (a) although HOLD-tightened-SL was equally valid since position was 4.7% < 5% rule-11 threshold

changes.expected_impact: Reduce avg-loss-per-close from current -₹1,448 lifetime / -₹1,988 today toward the SL-defined worst-case (typically 1.5-4% of position size with current SL band). Asymmetric loss-aversion (cut losers / hold winners) should resolve to symmetric SL discipline.

changes.title: EXIT IS FINAL — NO SECOND ORDER ON A CLOSED CNC POSITION (extended)
changes.summary: Extended Rule 12 from 'NO MODIFY ON CLOSED POSITIONS' to 'NO SECOND ORDER ON A CLOSED CNC POSITION' to also forbid a fresh CNC SELL on a SELL-to-zero symbol. Added explicit pointer that CNC SELL with zero holdings = guardrail rejection per SEBI rule 3. Bearish post-exit views require an explicit MIS short with same-day square-off, written as such.

blocked_orders.block_reason: Cannot short-sell in CNC. Holdings: 0, Order qty: 28
Cannot short-sell in CNC. Holdings: 0, Order qty: 1200
Cannot short-sell in CNC. Holdings: 0, Order qty: 28

evidence.context: All 3 blocked SELLs occurred WITHIN MINUTES of the same symbols' SELL-to-zero exits at 11:44:10 / 11:44:18 / 11:44:18. Missing stop_loss/target indicates stale-holdings read, not deliberate bearish flip. Prior Rule 12 only addressed MODIFY-after-EXIT (Day-3 failure mode); the same structural error in fresh-SELL syntax was not explicitly covered.

changes.expected_impact: Zero CNC short-sell guardrail rejections on subsequent down-days. Decision-tick cycles freed for legitimate position management.

changes.title: daily_loss_limit_pct override 0.075 → 0.04
changes.summary: Tightened the session-level circuit-breaker from base 7.5% to 4% via the hot-reload override path. Effective immediately for the next decision tick.

evidence.rationale: Base 7.5% would tolerate -₹75K of session-day loss before circuit-breaking — disproportionate to actual session-loss distribution (max -0.6%). 4% = -₹40K still gives 6.7x today's worst headroom but creates a real circuit at a level worth pausing for. Overrides only tighten; base config unchanged.

changes.expected_impact: On a future bad day where realized+unrealized loss approaches 4%, the system outputs NO_ACTION on all decisions, preventing further panic-exit cascades. Doesn't bind on today's pattern but creates a meaningful backstop.

changes_NOT_made_with_rationale.reason_skipped: Today's losers spanned 0.65 (HINDUNILVR) and 0.70 (TORNTPOWER, MAHABANK). Confidence has no signal at n=8 closed trades (3W/5L). Rotating this lever risks starving legitimate signal without addressing the actual failure mode (exit discipline, not entry quality). Today's COALINDIA BUY at 0.65 was clean and well-reasoned.

MAHABANK's grandfathered 9.78% × 6.13% SL distance = 0.60% per-name risk is the worst-case in the book and matches the current ceiling. Today's exits FREED 14.6 pp of deployment, so position-size is not the binding constraint — the failure was on the EXIT side.

Post-exit deployment is now 44.6%, well below the 65% cap. Tightening on a freed book would block legitimate entries (today's COALINDIA BUY would have been admitted under either threshold) without addressing the actual Day-6 failure mode.
Overrides have only 4 trading days of evidence. Promotion criterion should be ≥10 days plus statistical confirmation. Listed in recommendations for the next weekly review.

Engine doesn't currently support this param. Would require code change to GuardrailEngine. The behavioral fix at the prompt layer (rule 15) is sufficient for now; if pattern recurs, escalate to a code-level guardrail in a future review.

Today's exits dropped Power-sector exposure (TORNTPOWER closed). Energy/PSU is now: NTPC 5.05% + POWERGRID 4.81% + COALINDIA 4.69% + GOLDBEES 5.00% = ~19.5% — comfortable buffer to 22% cap. No firing trigger.
